jokes.text: I sponsored a kid from the inner city to go to summer camp. You sponsored an inner-city kid? An underprivileged kid. You're kidding. No. I'm completely nonplussed. Is that the right word?
jokes.setup: Larry reveals he did something charitable, shocking his friends
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Larry David sponsored a disadvantaged child is so out of character it leaves everyone speechless
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Larry doing something nice is genuinely shocking to everyone who knows him

jokes.text: He set fire to the canteen and his cabin.
jokes.setup: Larry's one good deed backfiring
jokes.punchline: The kid he sponsored burned down camp facilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Larry's misfortune - his one act of kindness results in property damage

jokes.text: You do one nice thing. I assume it's the first nice thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about good deeds backfiring
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's casual confirmation that this is literally Larry's first nice act
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Cheryl knows Larry so well she can casually roast him

jokes.text: I tried to stop a woman who was about to get hit by a car, I screamed, 'Watch out!' And she said, 'Don't you tell me what to do!'
jokes.setup: Larry defending his good deeds
jokes.punchline: Even trying to save someone's life gets him yelled at
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation about social interactions gone wrong - perfectly captures his worldview

jokes.text: I'm out of the nice now, after a fire.
jokes.setup: Discussion of giving up on helping people
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual declaration that arson has ended his charitable phase
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - 'out of the nice now, after a fire' is hilariously matter-of-fact

jokes.text: If you're gonna be a maniac, pyro is a bad maniac. Not a good maniac. That's not a good maniac.
jokes.setup: Discussing what kind of mental illness would have been preferable
jokes.punchline: Treating different types of mania like consumer choices to be evaluated
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic - ranking mental illnesses by convenience factor

jokes.text: I'm finally in the house that Jerry Seinfeld built. With his own hands, and some hammers. He actually worked on it, like Jimmy Carter.
jokes.setup: Dean being impressed by Larry's house
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous image of Jerry Seinfeld doing construction work
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor imagining celebrities doing manual labor

jokes.text: Is that Swiss coffee or vanilla bean? I think it's Chocolate Cho-Cho.
jokes.setup: Dean asking seriously about paint colors
jokes.punchline: Larry's obviously made-up paint name delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Larry's casual dismissiveness toward home decor pretension

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a divorce, I'm telling you. I can't take her anymore!
jokes.setup: Larry showing the problematic wire to neighbors
jokes.punchline: Larry's extreme overreaction to a minor aesthetic issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry escalation - threatening divorce over home aesthetics

jokes.text: Kismet. Isn't that what it's called?
jokes.setup: Dean trying to sound sophisticated about the coincidence
jokes.punchline: Dean's slightly pretentious use of 'kismet' in this mundane context
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy - Dean trying to sound cultured

jokes.text: A lot of people sue me. I'm very vulgar and... You might wind up suing me.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual acknowledgment of his lawsuit-prone personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-awareness - Larry knows exactly who he is

jokes.text: She's not just beautiful, and she's not just talented. She's sublime.
jokes.setup: Dean gushing about Julia Louis-Dreyfus
jokes.punchline: Dean's over-the-top worship of a TV actress
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - adult fan worship taken too far

jokes.text: Like you want to get something buried, you don't know if it's guaranteed. You don't even know if that... There's a lot of papers to be signed.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to make analogies about guarantees
jokes.punchline: Larry's transparent attempt to steer conversation back to his wire problem
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Larry's obvious agenda seeping through polite conversation

jokes.text: Fuck him!
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl being polite to the neighbors
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate explosive reaction once they're alone
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the contrast between public politeness and private fury

jokes.text: They'll talk on the phone, go out to dinner, go to the movies? Start e-mailing each other? Summer vacations? Is he out of his fucking mind?
jokes.setup: Larry analyzing Dean's fantasy about meeting Julia
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating list of imagined friendship activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation building to 'summer vacations' - perfectly captures fan delusion

jokes.text: One phone call, one small favor to make this house completely perfect.
jokes.setup: Larry's massive rant about Dean's unreasonable request
jokes.punchline: Cheryl dismissing his concerns as 'one small favor'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Larry's hysteria and Cheryl's pragmatic dismissal. Shows their different priorities.

jokes.text: 10:00 is the cut-off time. You don't call people after 10:00. No, the cut-off time is 10:30. They have kids. You don't call after 10:00. No, 10:30.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl debating phone etiquette
jokes.punchline: The setup for Larry's later mistake about call times
jokes.explanation: Important setup for future payoffs - establishing the phone call time rules

jokes.text: How'd that work out for you?
jokes.setup: Larry calling after 10:00 and getting yelled at
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's sarcastic response to Larry's mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect 'I told you so' moment with excellent economy

jokes.text: You've got me some expensive idiot lawyer, so you can have your wire dropped down. I know your games.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about the lawyer Larry recommended
jokes.punchline: Jeff's paranoid theory about Larry's wire-related conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid logic connecting the two plot threads

jokes.text: You want a prick, and this guy's a prick. Look at you. You're a prick, you're doing a good job. Are you a prick or not?
jokes.setup: Larry defending his choice of lawyer
jokes.punchline: Larry's logic that being a prick is a professional qualification
jokes.explanation: Great Larry logic - turning personality flaws into professional assets

jokes.text: He burned down the canteen, and the cabin, but they were both accidents.
jokes.setup: Jeff defending the kid who caused the fire
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of accidentally burning down two separate buildings
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - multiple 'accidental' arsons

jokes.text: Get the kid to burn my house, so I don't have to worry about the wire.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the arsonist kid staying with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Larry's suggestion to use arson to solve his wire problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy connecting both plot threads - Larry's willingness to commit insurance fraud

jokes.text: My cat died.
jokes.setup: Julia asking if Phyllis is okay
jokes.punchline: The awkward revelation that derails the entire meeting
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup - inappropriate oversharing derailing the meeting

jokes.text: And its tail had been trapped when the gardener came over... It had been trapped in the lawnmower...
jokes.setup: Phyllis beginning to share details about her cat
jokes.punchline: The increasingly graphic and inappropriate details
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe escalation - TMI about pet death

jokes.text: I don't wanna talk about Mittens. I've got a better idea. Hey, brainstorm. Phyllis has just had a complete... I don't know why I didn't think of this. Camcorder.
jokes.setup: Phyllis being upset about her cat
jokes.punchline: Her sudden pivot to wanting to film Julia Louis-Dreyfus
jokes.explanation: Good character work - fan obsession overriding grief

jokes.text: Give me another 10 minutes with this lunatic. She's a lunatic. She's a total lunatic.
jokes.setup: Julia dealing with Phyllis's bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: Julia's increasingly frustrated assessment of Phyllis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Julia's frustration

jokes.text: He's a eunuch. Yes. His testicles were cut off when he was about 13 because he was in the Beth Shalom choir. And that's what he wanted to be, he was a choirboy.
jokes.setup: Phyllis asking about Jerry Seinfeld on camera
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely fabricated and bizarre story about Jerry
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and dark - Larry creating an insane lie about his famous friend

jokes.text: You know, I've got to tell you, I don't have any fucking idea.
jokes.setup: Phyllis asking Julia to confirm Larry's story about Jerry
jokes.punchline: Julia's exasperated honest response
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Larry's absurd lie - Julia's breaking point

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing over there? He's supposed to be here! I can't kick him out, we've got business to do. What do you mean? I've got Julia Louis-Dreyfus in his house waiting for him!
jokes.setup: Larry calling Jeff about Dean not being home
jokes.punchline: The complete breakdown of Larry's carefully orchestrated plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's frustration as his plans collapse

jokes.text: I was gonna get this. I've never turned a piece of jewelry around this fast.
jokes.setup: Phyllis showing Julia jewelry
jokes.punchline: Phyllis bragging about her sales skills to a celebrity customer
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Phyllis treating Julia like any customer

jokes.text: I guess Julia will have to come back over. You'll have to arrange another meeting. Are you out of your mind now? Get Julia back over there? You think I'm gonna ask her again to do this?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggesting they reschedule with Julia
jokes.punchline: Larry's explosive rejection of asking for another favor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's awareness of friendship boundaries

jokes.text: I'm not pushing, 2 minutes is pushing. 10 minutes is not pushing. That's not a push. That is right in the wheelhouse. I have 10 minutes before the cut-off time.
jokes.setup: Larry justifying calling Julia before the phone cut-off time
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly technical analysis of phone calling timing using baseball metaphors
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the phone timing obsession with Larry's characteristic over-analysis

jokes.text: I got to tell you, I never heard of a 9:30 cut-off.
jokes.setup: Larry calling Julia and being told he's too late
jokes.punchline: Larry's disbelief at the early phone cutoff time
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier phone etiquette debate

jokes.text: Most people have a 10:00 or 10:30 cut-off. I think 9:30 with kids is great. Seems like an early cut-off to me. And for visitors, it's more like 9:00.
jokes.setup: Larry debating phone etiquette with Julia's husband
jokes.punchline: The escalating restrictions on when you can visit or call
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of social etiquette rules getting more restrictive

jokes.text: Most people have a 10:00 or 10:30 cut-off. I think 9:30 with kids is great. Seems like an early cut-off to me. It's pretty standard. And for visitors, it's more like 9:00.
jokes.setup: Larry and Brad debating phone cut-off times
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific phone etiquette rules, ending with different standards for visitors
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of the phone timing debate with escalating complexity

jokes.text: Most people have a 10:00 or 10:30 cut-off. I think 9:30 with kids is great. Seems like an early cut-off to me.
jokes.setup: Brad explaining their 9:30 cut-off time
jokes.punchline: Larry's diplomatic disagreement while still defending his position
jokes.explanation: Continues callback theme with Larry still defending his position despite being wrong multiple times.

jokes.text: And for visitors, it's more like 9:00.
jokes.setup: Discussion of call-time cut-offs
jokes.punchline: Even earlier cut-off for visitors makes Larry's visit inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Escalates the social rule violation - Larry is breaking multiple boundaries simultaneously.

jokes.text: We'll look carefully tomorrow, Larry. And those are place mats.
jokes.setup: Larry searching through Julia's kitchen drawers
jokes.punchline: Julia's patient but strained attempts to stop Larry from ransacking her house
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Larry's obliviousness to boundaries

jokes.text: Did you find a little brown pad, it has a little black trimming on the side?
jokes.setup: Larry waking up Julia's child while searching
jokes.punchline: Larry asking the child about his pad while the parents try to get rid of him
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe - Larry involving children in his obsessive search

jokes.text: I just can't believe that I have to deal with this! He has ransacked our entire house! Do you know what he took, Jeffrey? Do you wanna know what this little shit took?
jokes.setup: Susie discovering items missing from her house
jokes.punchline: Susie's explosive rage at the theft
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the kid's crimes getting worse

jokes.text: Everything's gone! My wedding video is gone because your fat-fuck manager over here let an asshole kid into our house, okay?
jokes.setup: Susie listing what was stolen
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of missing items including irreplaceable memories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of consequences - from fire to theft

jokes.text: What the fuck was that about, calling at 10:20 at night the other day? I thought the cut-off was 10:30. You know I have trouble sleeping?
jokes.setup: Susie confronting Larry about his late phone call
jokes.punchline: The callback to the phone time debate with additional stakes
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalated stakes - now we know why the time matters

jokes.text: Who is this schmuck, fuck-face lawyer you set us up with? I could see how one could interpret that as a serious error in judgment.
jokes.setup: Susie asking about the lawyer Larry recommended
jokes.punchline: Larry's diplomatic acknowledgment of his mistake
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character work - Larry's understated admission of guilt

jokes.text: She brought it over from Russia, from a pogrom. She was in steerage. If that's gone, I'm gonna be hysterical. She worked hard all her life to give that to me!
jokes.setup: Susie describing her grandmother's brooch
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top emotional significance of the stolen item
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making the theft more emotionally devastating

jokes.text: It's gone, you fucking, motherfucking... I'll kill you, Jeff! You're a cocksucker, motherfucker! My grandmother's brooch is missing. I could not replace this item.
jokes.setup: Susie discovering the brooch is missing
jokes.punchline: Susie's complete meltdown with escalating profanity
jokes.explanation: Peak Susie rage - perfect character integration and escalation

jokes.text: What if something you cared about was missing? You don't give a shit about anything! My baseball cards. All right. See? Now, it's not so funny anymore. Mickey Mantle might not be there.
jokes.setup: Susie asking Larry to empathize
jokes.punchline: Larry's childish comparison to baseball cards
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Larry's inability to grasp emotional significance

jokes.text: Like the fucking 3-year-old that you are. 'I don't care about the money.' Will you shut up? Fuck you!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to console Susie about the theft
jokes.punchline: Susie's perfect takedown of Larry's childish response with voice mimicry
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Susie's ability to cut through Larry's deflection

jokes.text: Like the fucking 3-year-old that you are. 'I don't care about the money.'
jokes.setup: Larry's baseball card comparison
jokes.punchline: Susie's devastating assessment of Larry's maturity level
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character assassination with perfect age comparison. Susie cuts right to Larry's core immaturity.

jokes.text: Jesus Christ. This looks bad, I know it does. This is my bracelet. Cheryl bought the same bracelet that you-- Julia, I swear to you, I didn't--
jokes.setup: Larry appearing at Julia's house with an identical bracelet
jokes.punchline: The impossible-to-believe coincidence that makes Larry look guilty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup - the perfect storm of circumstantial evidence against Larry

jokes.text: You were supposed to come the other day, and you didn't show up? Okay, can I tell you something, my friend? When you make appointments, keep it! Because now I'm busy, and I can't talk to you anymore. Keep your appointments.
jokes.setup: Dean finally meeting Julia Louis-Dreyfus
jokes.punchline: Julia lecturing Dean about keeping appointments
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Dean gets his dream meeting as a scolding

jokes.text: That was maybe an encounter, but that's not a meeting. I don't know why you're quibbling about semantics here. You met her. It was an unpleasant meeting, it wasn't a long meeting, but it was, nevertheless, a meeting.
jokes.setup: Dean complaining that his meeting with Julia wasn't what he wanted
jokes.punchline: Larry's lawyerly argument about what constitutes a meeting
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Larry's legalistic approach to social interactions

jokes.text: There's a reward involved in that. Look right in the front part. It says, '$500 to the person who returns this to L. David.' I put two and two together and thought the 'L' stood for Larry.
jokes.setup: Dean returning Larry's lost pad
jokes.punchline: Dean discovering and claiming the reward Larry wrote in his pad
jokes.explanation: Great callback payoff - Larry's desperation creating his own financial obligation

jokes.text: Maybe you can get yourself a new sweater. It's 'E-I.' Weinstock.
jokes.setup: Larry having to pay Dean the reward money
jokes.punchline: Larry's petty insult about Dean's sweater and spelling correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pettiness - unable to graciously pay what he owes

jokes.text: I'll get a check, Sherlock. Perfect, check is fine. Maybe you can get yourself a new sweater.
jokes.setup: Dean expecting payment for 'finding' the pad
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic 'Sherlock' and petty insult about Dean's sweater
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment with Larry's characteristic petty insult after being extorted
